notes: Machine-readable list of agent-ready tasks for Commonweave. Pick one. See AGENTS.md for workflow.

tasks.title: Verify one underrepresented country
tasks.goal: Pick one country with fewer than 50 organizations in the directory (most of the 172 countries qualify) and verify or improve at least 10 records.
acceptance_criteria: At least 10 organizations checked
Broken links corrected or marked
Misclassified records corrected
No vulnerable group exposed unnecessarily
PR includes a short methodology note (what was checked, how, with what sources)

tasks.title: Add high-confidence-only map toggle
tasks.goal: Add a map toggle that filters to Tier B organizations with alignment_score >= 5 (~10,824 of ~27,217 geocoded points currently shown).
acceptance_criteria: Toggle is visible on desktop and mobile
Default map behavior remains unchanged
Filtered mode explains what high-confidence means in 1-2 sentences
Map still loads without console errors

tasks.title: Add provenance metadata to map edges
tasks.goal: Update generated edges in data/build_map_v2.py to include edge_type, confidence, explanation, created_at, and source_script.
acceptance_criteria: Edge schema includes edge_type, confidence, explanation, created_at, source_script
Map can display or inspect edge explanation (hover, click, or panel)
Build script documents how confidence is assigned
Existing map data remains backward compatible (no broken renders)

tasks.title: Backfill legibility for one country
tasks.goal: Classify records in one country as formal, hybrid, informal, or unknown. The legibility column was added 2026-04-22; as of 2026-04-25, ~25,980 records still read 'unknown' and ~138,803 read 'formal' (auto-classified at ingest time). Either subset is worth working in -- pick a country and audit/backfill there.
acceptance_criteria: At least 20 records reviewed
Each classification has an evidence note in the PR or remains unknown
Informal/vulnerable groups handled conservatively (default to unknown if any doubt)
PR explains classification method

tasks.title: Add one serious falsifier
tasks.goal: Pick one claim in README.md or DEEP-DIVE.md and define what evidence would weaken or falsify it.
acceptance_criteria: Claim is quoted or referenced precisely (file + section)
Falsifier is concrete and observable
At least one source or real-world case is cited
PR explains whether the claim should be kept, weakened, or marked unresolved

tasks.title: Fill one [NEEDS EXAMPLE] cell
tasks.goal: The governance matrix in README.md has cells marked [NEEDS EXAMPLE]. Pick one. Find a real organization (preferably already in the directory) that fits. Cite source.
acceptance_criteria: One cell filled with a named organization
Source cited (URL, registry, news, or peer-reviewed)
1-2 sentence explanation of why it fits
If org isn't in the directory, link to where it should be added

tasks.title: Seed CLAIMS.md with 10 claims from README
tasks.goal: Extract at least 10 specific claims from README.md and add them to CLAIMS.md with status, confidence, evidence (or lack of), and falsifier ideas.
acceptance_criteria: At least 10 claims extracted, each tied to a README section
Each has status (draft/supported/disputed/unresolved)
Each has confidence (low/medium/high)
Do not overstate certainty
At least 3 claims include concrete falsifiers

tasks.title: Document at least 3 attack vectors
tasks.goal: Add at least 3 attack vectors to ATTACK-VECTORS.md (e.g. corporate capture, state surveillance, data extraction, founder bottleneck, spam flooding, ideological hardening).
acceptance_criteria: Each vector has: threat, who benefits, who is harmed, early warning signs, countermeasures, open questions
Vectors are realistic, not paranoid
Cross-references THREAT-MODEL.md where relevant

tasks.title: Improve mobile map controls
tasks.goal: Make map.html usable on a phone. Touch-friendly filter panel, better popups, no horizontal overflow.
acceptance_criteria: Filter panel is reachable and operable on a 375px-wide screen
Popups don't overflow viewport
No horizontal scroll on the page
Existing desktop behavior unchanged

tasks.title: Create llms.txt and llms-full.txt at repo root
tasks.goal: Add a curated llms.txt index pointing to README, CRITIQUE, AGENTS, AGENT-TASKS, data contributing guide, map, directory. Add a longer llms-full.txt digest.
acceptance_criteria: llms.txt is a short curated index following the llmstxt.org spec
llms-full.txt contains a longer machine-readable digest
Both link to canonical site URLs (commonweave.earth or simonlpaige.com/commonweave/) where applicable
Both are kept short enough to be useful (no whole-file dumps)
